name: Vilalba Sasserra, El Far, and Turó de les Puces Loop
name_original: Palautordera, El Far, Turo de las Hormigas - circular
description: This moderate 16.7-mile mountain biking loop from Palautordera explores varied terrain and historical sites in Parc del Montnegre i el Corre
region_intro: Mountain biking around Santa Maria de Palautordera offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Parc Natural del Montseny, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. The region is characterized by extensive forest tracks, significant mountain passes, and varied ecosystems, transitioning from Mediterranean holm oak forests to Central European oak and beech forests. Prominent peaks like Turó de l'Home (1,706 m) and Les Agudes (1,703 m) provide considerable elevation changes, while the Tordera River valley features lush natural scenery. This terrain provides a range of mountain bike trails suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Santa Maria De Palautordera The most popular mountain bike trail is Vilalba Sasserra, El Far, and Turó de les Puces Loop, a 16.7 miles (26.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 18 minutes to complete. This moderate route navigates varied terrain with views of the surrounding hills. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Coll Formic Pass (1,145 m) – Pla de la Calma loop from Santa Maria de Palautordera, a difficult 31.1 miles (50.0 km) path. This challenging route features significant elevation gain through the Montseny Massif. Local mountain bikers also love the Sant Martí de Montnegre Church – Pega Kilns, Can Valls loop from Palautordera, a 29.9 miles (48.1 km) trail leading through forest tracks and past historical sites, often completed in about 4 hours 3 minutes. Mountain biking around Santa Maria De Palautordera is defined by extensive forest tracks, significant mountain passes, and diverse forest types. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult routes. The routes in Santa Maria De Palautordera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Santa Maria De Palautordera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Santa Maria de Palautordera, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: There are 30 mountain bike trails around Santa Maria de Palautordera. These include 4 easy routes, 17 moderate routes, and 9 difficult routes, offering options for various skill levels.

Q: What types of terrain and natural features can I expect on the mountain bike trails in the Montseny area?
A: The trails in the Montseny area offer a rich and diverse landscape. You'll encounter extensive forest tracks, significant mountain passes, and varied ecosystems, transitioning from Mediterranean holm oak forests at lower altitudes to Central European oak and beech forests higher up. The terrain can range from smooth paths to sections with steep climbs, rocks, and roots, providing a varied riding experience.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 9 difficult routes available. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19195105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll Formic Pass (1,145 m) – Pla de la Calma loop from Santa Maria de Palautordera&lt;/a&gt;, a 31.1-mile (50.0 km) path featuring significant elevation gain through the Montseny Massif.

Q: What are some moderate mountain bike trails in the area?
A: The area offers 17 moderate mountain bike trails. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31469341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Martí de Montnegre Church – Pega Kilns, Can Valls loop from Palautordera&lt;/a&gt;, a 29.9-mile (48.1 km) route that takes you through forest tracks and past historical sites. Another good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17562901" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cedro de Can Valls – Pega Kilns, Can Valls loop from Palautordera&lt;/a&gt;, covering 39.4 miles (39.4 km) with notable elevation changes.

Q: What landmarks or points of interest can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can encounter several landmarks. These include historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/132844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Pedra Gentil&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1296433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Ca l'Arenes&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass by significant peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1687529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó de l'Home (Man's Hill)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/988421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Agudes&lt;/a&gt;, offering impressive views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/885563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallforners Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is another scenic spot.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in the area are circular. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19195105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll Formic Pass (1,145 m) – Pla de la Calma loop from Santa Maria de Palautordera&lt;/a&gt; is a circular route. Another moderate circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/16522685" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Polell Rural Hostel – Hermitage of Sant Elies (Saint Elias) loop from Santa Maria de Palautordera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Santa Maria de Palautordera?
A: The mountain bike trails around Santa Maria de Palautordera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 60 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, from extensive forest tracks to challenging mountain passes, and the beautiful natural scenery of the Parc Natural del Montseny.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the mountain bike trailheads?
A: Santa Maria de Palautordera is situated in the Tordera River valley, offering good access. While specific public transport links to individual trailheads may vary, the town itself serves as a gateway to the Montseny Natural Park, and local transport options can help you get closer to starting points.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Santa Maria de Palautordera?
A: The region's diverse climate, transitioning from Mediterranean to Central European, means mountain biking is enjoyable across many seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, especially at lower altitudes, while winter might bring cooler conditions, particularly in the higher elevations of Montseny.

Q: Are there any permits required for mountain biking in the Parc Natural del Montseny?
A: As the Parc Natural del Montseny is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, it's advisable to check the official regulations regarding mountain biking. While general access is often permitted on designated trails, specific rules or permits might apply for certain areas or activities to protect the natural environment. Always respect local signage and conservation efforts.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in the area?
A: Yes, among the 30 routes, there are 4 easy trails that are generally more suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride. These routes typically feature smoother paths and less significant elevation changes, allowing for a more relaxed experience amidst the natural beauty.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails?
A: Many natural parks, including the Parc Natural del Montseny, have specific regulations regarding dogs to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of all visitors. It's recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas, and to check the park's official guidelines for any specific restrictions on trails or during certain seasons.
tips:
In the Vallès Oriental region and the Baix Montseny subregion, there is the municipality of Vilalba Sasserra, in the Mogent Valley. Formerly it was known as Trentapasses or thirty steps, the distance that separates it from the neighboring town of Llinars del Vallès.

The 4,000-year-old Pedra Arca dolmen is the oldest archaeological site in the municipality. The old church of Santa María, in Romanesque style, and the many scattered farmhouses (Can Gol, Can Esteve, Can Agell, Can Bosch, etc.) are the best examples of municipal heritage.

Plaça de la Vila, in the center of the historic complex, is home to the Vilalba Sasserra Town Hall. It is a building of contemporary design, with a rectangular and horizontal structure, made up of two concrete blocks that form an angle. The glass windows are also rectangular.
In Sant Andreu del Far you will find a Gothic church and a farmhouse restaurant. It's a good place to stop for a photo or even a meal.
A beautiful road with many curves in the forest. Easy and fun in both directions.
This path is perfect for avoiding roads and going under the highway.
A forest road with trees and perfect for walking and cycling.

faqs.question: How long does this mountain bike route typically take to complete?
faqs.answer: This circular mountain bike route is approximately 26.9 km (16.7 miles) long and generally takes around 2 hours and 18 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as moderate in difficulty. While it's a circular trail, it involves an elevation gain of about 484 meters (1,588 feet), which might be challenging for absolute beginners. Riders with some mountain biking experience and a reasonable fitness level should find it enjoyable.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Palautordera, making it convenient for a circular ride. While specific trailhead parking isn't detailed, Santa Maria de Palautordera is a town with general parking options. It's advisable to look for public parking areas within the town before starting your ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, typical of the Montseny Natural Park foothills. You can expect a mix of dirt tracks, gravel roads, and potentially some singletrack sections. The route will likely pass through forested areas and offer views of the Montseny massif, with some ascents and descents.

faqs.question: What points of interest can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes by several interesting spots. You'll encounter the Old Church of Santa María of Vilalba Sasserra and the Sant Andreu del Far Church. You'll also ride along scenic sections like the Trail from Far to Can Guinard Forest, the Connection Path to the Mogent River, and the Forest Trail Between Línea de Avi and Turó de las Hormigas.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other established trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Sender de la Tordera al Llobregat, Catalunya E15, GR 92 BTT, Montgat-Tordera, TransCatalunya. De la Mediterrània, and Tourdera trails.

faqs.question: Is the Palautordera, El Far, Turo de las Hormigas circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in this region, including those within Parc del Montnegre i el Corredor, are dog-friendly. However, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always carry waste bags and clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this route in Parc del Montnegre i el Corredor?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or specific permits required for mountain biking on public trails within Parc del Montnegre i el Corredor. However, always respect park regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of the natural environment.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to ride this trail are generally spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is usually milder, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially wet conditions, making some sections muddy.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or pubs nearby in Palautordera?
faqs.answer: Yes, Santa Maria de Palautordera is a town with various amenities. You'll find cafes, restaurants, and pubs where you can refuel and relax before or after your ride. It's a good idea to check opening hours, especially if you're riding early or late.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: For a moderate mountain bike route of this length, it's recommended to bring plenty of water, snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), a first-aid kit, and appropriate clothing for the weather. A helmet is essential, and consider bringing a GPS device or a fully charged phone with the komoot app for navigation.
